# UC-14.6.11 · EtherNet/IP CIP Service Request Audit

## Description

Unusual CIP services (e.g., configuration writes) against controllers can precede firmware or logic manipulation; service baselines highlight drift from normal automation behavior.

## Value

Unusual CIP services (e.g., configuration writes) against controllers can precede firmware or logic manipulation; service baselines highlight drift from normal automation behavior.

## Implementation

Deploy ICSNPP-ENIP on Zeek at CIP/EtherNet/IP taps (ports 2222/44818 per policy). Build per-controller service profiles; alert on rare services or configuration-class access from non-engineering hosts.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:enip:json"` (`cip_service`, `cip_service_code`, `class_name`, `direction`, `source_h`, `destination_h`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy ICSNPP-ENIP on Zeek at CIP/EtherNet/IP taps (ports 2222/44818 per policy). Build per-controller service profiles; alert on rare services or configuration-class access from non-engineering hosts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:enip:json" direction="Request"
| stats count by cip_service class_name source_h destination_h
| eventstats sum(count) as svc_total by destination_h
| eval pct=round(100*count/svc_total,3)
| where cip_service IN ("Set_Attribute_Single","Reset","Delete") OR pct<0.1
| sort destination_h - pct
| table destination_h cip_service class_name count pct source_h
```

Understanding this SPL

**EtherNet/IP CIP Service Request Audit** — Unusual CIP services (e.g., configuration writes) against controllers can precede firmware or logic manipulation; service baselines highlight drift from normal automation behavior.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:enip:json"` (`cip_service`, `cip_service_code`, `class_name`, `direction`, `source_h`, `destination_h`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: zeek:enip:json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="zeek:enip:json". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cip_service class_name source_h destination_h** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by destination_h** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where cip_service IN ("Set_Attribute_Single","Reset","Delete") OR pct<0.1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **EtherNet/IP CIP Service Request Audit**): table destination_h cip_service class_name count pct source_h

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a short window and compare key fields with Zeek or Corelight protocol logs on the sensor for the same flow. Reconcile function codes, endpoints, and time order against a small known-good capture if needed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (CIP service mix), Table (rare services), Heatmap (service by source).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:enip:json" direction="Request"
| stats count by cip_service class_name source_h destination_h
| eventstats sum(count) as svc_total by destination_h
| eval pct=round(100*count/svc_total,3)
| where cip_service IN ("Set_Attribute_Single","Reset","Delete") OR pct<0.1
| sort destination_h - pct
| table destination_h cip_service class_name count pct source_h
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (CIP service mix), Table (rare services), Heatmap (service by source).
